summary: I was born in Crescent City, California, a small town in northern California. It's where my story begins before my family moved to Seattle. Although I didn't grow up there, Crescent City is significant as my birthplace and the starting point of my journey as part of the Hmong immigrant experience.

I was raised in Seattle, Washington, and that's where my family still lives today. Growing up there shaped much of my early life and identity. Seattle represents the place where my family settled and where I spent my formative years before moving to St. Paul.

Now, I live in St. Paul, Minnesota, where I also went to school. St. Paul is where I pursued my education and continue to build my life. It’s a place that represents my growth and the next chapter beyond my upbringing in Seattle.

I attended Concordia University here in St. Paul, where I furthered my education. Going to Concordia was an important step for me as part of my journey as a Hmong immigrant in the United States. It’s where I gained knowledge and experiences that helped shape who I am today.
